title: scalesia affinis subsp. affinis
intro: Scalesia affinis subsp. affinis, also known as the lesser Scalesia, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Galapagos Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, Scalesia affinis subsp. affinis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its uses and preparation methods require further research and validation.
